perturbation.original_text: This Schedule ("Schedule") is made as of the effective date set forth above to that Software License, Customization, and Maintenance Agreement, by and between Cardlytics, Inc. ("Supplier") and Bank of America, N. A, ("Bank of America"), dated November 5, 2010, as amended ("SLCMA"). Each capitalized term used but not defined herein shall have the meaning assigned in the SLCMA.
perturbation.changed_text: This Schedule ("Schedule") is made as of the effective date set forth above to that Software License, Customization, and Maintenance Agreement, by and between Cardlytics, Inc. ("Supplier") and Bank of America, N. A, ("Bank of America"), dated November 5, 2010, as amended ("SLCMA"). Each capitalized term used but not defined herein shall have the meaning assigned in this Schedule.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that terms not defined in the Schedule should refer to the schedule itself, where these terms would never be defined, when the previous sentence explicitly defines that the terms are in the SLCMA. This creates confusion as the SLCMA actually contain these definitions, leading to disputes regarding their interpretation and which source should be consulted. It contradicts the understanding of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Schedule ("Schedule") is made as of the effective date set forth above to that Software License, Customization, and Maintenance Agreement, by and between Cardlytics, Inc. ("Supplier") and Bank of America, N. A, ("Bank of America"), dated November 5, 2010, as amended ("SLCMA"). Each capitalized term used but not defined herein shall have the meaning assigned in this Schedule.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that undefined capitalized terms within the Schedule should be interpreted by referencing the Schedule itself, where such terms are never defined. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text which also states that undefined terms in the Schedule should refer to the Schedule itself, which creates a logical inconsistency, making it impossible to understand undefined terms within the schedule. The original text, in contrast, stipulated that undefined terms should refer to the SLCMA, which actually contains those definitions. This change directly contradicts the original intent and creates a clear inconsistency, leading to potential disputes over interpretation.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, Bank of America and Supplier entered into the SLCMA in order to set forth the terms and conditions pursuant to which Supplier provides certain Software to Bank of America, WHEREAS, the parties desire to add to the SLCMA the Supplier Offer Placement System Software; NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the promises and accords made herein, and the exchange of such good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, Bank of America and Supplier agree as follows: The attached Schedule [A] is hereby incorporated into the SLCMA describing the Offer Placement System Software for use by Bank of America.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, Bank of America and Supplier entered into the SLCMA in order to set forth the terms and conditions pursuant to which Supplier provides certain Software to Bank of America, WHEREAS, the parties desire to replace the SLCMA with the Supplier Offer Placement System Software; NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the promises and accords made herein, and the exchange of such good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, Bank of America and Supplier agree as follows: The attached Schedule [A] is hereby incorporated into the SLCMA describing the Offer Placement System Software for use by Bank of America.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text indicates that the schedule is being added to the existing Software License, Customization, and Maintenance Agreement (SLCMA). The modified text, however, states that the parties desire to replace the SLCMA with the Supplier Offer Placement System Software. This directly contradicts the original intent of merely adding to the existing agreement versus substituting it entirely. This would cause a dispute on which parts of the previous agreement are still valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, Bank of America and Supplier entered into the SLCMA in order to set forth the terms and conditions pursuant to which Supplier provides certain Software to Bank of America, WHEREAS, the parties desire to replace the SLCMA with the Supplier Offer Placement System Software; NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the promises and accords made herein, and the exchange of such good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, Bank of America and Supplier agree as follows: The attached Schedule [A] is hereby incorporated into the SLCMA describing the Offer Placement System Software for use by Bank of America.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the parties desire to 'replace' the SLCMA, while the contradicted text refers to adding Schedule [A] to the existing SLCMA. These are directly contradictory; one clause indicates the agreement is being replaced, and the other assumes it is being supplemented, creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
